text:
Christmas, holiday originating in Christianity, usually December 25, also known as Xmas, X-mas, Christmas celebration. Attributes include: 
- foods traditionally associated: "gingerbread",
 "Christmas pudding",
 "turrón",
 "polvoron (country: Spain)",
 "turkey meat (country: United Kingdom)",
 "Christmas ham (country: Sweden)",
 "meatball (country: Sweden)",
 "pinnekjøtt (country: Norway)",
 "medisterpølse (country: Norway)",
 "pork belly (country: Norway)",
 "smalahove (country: Norway)"
- instance of: "public holiday (applies to jurisdiction: United Kingdom, Sweden, Australia)",
 "federal holiday in the United States"
- Commons category: "Christmas"
- Commons gallery: "Christmas"
- day in year for periodic occurrence: "December 25 (part of: Eastern Orthodox liturgical calendar ; liturgical rank: Great Feasts of the Orthodox Church)"
- described by source: "The Nuttall Encyclopædia (subject named as: Christmas)",
 "Brockhaus and Efron Encyclopedic Dictionary",
 "The Catholic Encyclopedia",
 "Zedler, Großes vollständiges Universallexicon aller Wissenschaften und Künste"
- commemorates: "Nativity of Jesus"
- color: "white (statement is subject of: liturgical colour)"
- hashtag: "Christmas (language of work or name: English)",
 "Xmas (language of work or name: English)",
 "Weihnachten (language of work or name: German)"
- has part(s): "Christmas Eve",
 "Saint Stephen's Day",
 "Second Day of Christmas",
 "Boxing Day"
- part of: "Great Feasts of the Orthodox Church",
 "Christmastide"
- maintained by WikiProject: "WikiProject Christianity",
